observation: missing fire extinguisher in haul truck
reasoningExpectation: Fire extinguisher required on self-propelled mobile equipment under 30 CFR 56.4100 or 56.9100. Machine guarding is completely irrelevant.

observation: unguarded conveyor tail pulley
reasoningExpectation: Moving machine parts require guarding under 30 CFR 56.14107(a) to protect miners from contact with rotating components.

observation: exposed live electrical wiring on packaging machine
reasoningExpectation: Live electrical parts must be enclosed under 1910.303(b)(1) to prevent accidental contact and electrocution hazards.

observation: open floor edge without guardrails or fall arrest at six feet height
reasoningExpectation: OSHA Construction requires fall protection for workers exposed to falls of 6 feet or more under 29 CFR 1926.501(b)(1).

observation: unlabeled chemical storage tank containing corrosive liquid
reasoningExpectation: Hazard communication standard requires secondary and storage containers to be labeled with chemical identity under 1910.1200.

observation: performing cleanout of crusher drive belt pulley while machine is running without lockout applied
reasoningExpectation: OSHA lockout/tagout standard 1910.147 requires isolating and locking energy sources before servicing or maintenance.

observation: unauthorized employee entry into permit required confined space sewer tank without supervisor or attendant present
reasoningExpectation: Permit-required confined spaces must have entry procedures, atmospheric testing, and an active attendant under 1910.146.

observation: missing steel toe boots and hard hat in active warehouse loading dock area with heavy forklift traffic
reasoningExpectation: Employers must provide and ensure the use of appropriate personal protective equipment under 1910.132(a) where hazards are present.

observation: spillage of fine coal material and mud accumulation on transfer tower travelway catwalk
reasoningExpectation: MSHA housekeeping standard 30 CFR 56.20003 requires all walkways and travelways to be kept clean, orderly, and dry.

observation: blocked emergency exit door locked from the inside in packaging bay area during active shift
reasoningExpectation: Emergency exit routes must remain unlocked and free of obstructions under 1910.37 to guarantee safe egress.

observation: workers digging an eight-foot deep excavation trench without shoring or sloping system in sandy clay soil
reasoningExpectation: Trench excavations deeper than five feet must have protective systems like shoring or trench boxes under 29 CFR 1926.652.

observation: damaged and frayed wire rope sling used for overhead crane steel plate lifting
reasoningExpectation: Rigging equipment must be inspected daily and removed immediately if frayed, kinked, or damaged under 1910.184.

observation: unsecured gas cylinder standing upright without chain or cap near oxygen manifold
reasoningExpectation: Compressed gas cylinders must be secured upright in chains or carts with protective caps installed under 1910.101.

observation: operator operating forklift without wearing seatbelt near pedestrian walkway intersection
reasoningExpectation: Forklift operators must utilize seatbelts and sound horns at blind intersections under 29 CFR 1910.178.

observation: haul truck backup alarm inoperative while operating in pit loading area
reasoningExpectation: MSHA regulations 30 CFR 56.9100 require all mobile equipment to have working backup alarms and warning devices.

observation: visible thick white dust cloud from quarry secondary impact crusher circuit
reasoningExpectation: Visible respirable dust must be controlled using local exhaust or water sprays to prevent silicosis under 30 CFR 56.5000.

observation: combustible wood shavings and dust piled on top of electrical conduit and junction boxes in mill room
reasoningExpectation: Combustible dust accumulations must be cleaned from areas near ignition sources or electrical enclosures under 1910.22.

observation: lack of safety chain or secure wire rope connector on high pressure air line from mobile compressor
reasoningExpectation: High-pressure hoses must have safety chains or whipcheck devices to prevent whipping in case of connection failure under 30 CFR 56.13021.

observation: damaged structural rungs on portable aluminum extension ladder leaning against scaffold frame
reasoningExpectation: Defective ladders must be tagged 'Do Not Use' and immediately removed from service under 29 CFR 1926.1053.

observation: machine operator bypassed safety light curtain on milling machine using reflective tape to expedite feeding
reasoningExpectation: Safety interlocks, guards, and light curtains must not be bypassed or made inoperative under 29 CFR 1910.212.

observation: the operator of the packaging machine reported that the emergency stop button was not functioning
reasoningExpectation: Machine emergency stop controls must be active and tested under 1910.212. It should not map to building emergency egress.

observation: truck driver reported missing seatbelt buckle assembly inside the cab of the utility vehicle
reasoningExpectation: Seatbelts are a mobile equipment emergency and operating control standard under 30 CFR 56.9100, not personal protective equipment or machine guarding.

observation: chemical reaction vessel cleanout requires worker entry but the electrical lock box was damaged
reasoningExpectation: Cleanout requiring entry is primarily a Confined Space permit space entry (1910.146) with LOTO (1910.147) isolation, not standard Electrical installation.

observation: fire in the computer server cabinet was extinguished but the cooling fan guards were not reinstalled
reasoningExpectation: Missing cooling fan guards are a machine guarding exposure (1910.212) even though a fire event previously occurred. Do not map exclusively to fire standards.

observation: catwalk handrail loose near the belt conveyor drive motor assembly
reasoningExpectation: Catwalk handrails are fall protection structures under 30 CFR 56.11012. The proximity to the belt conveyor must not trigger machine guarding.

observation: electrical panel clear working space obstructed by heavy wooden shipping crates
reasoningExpectation: Obstruction of clear working space in front of electrical panels is an electrical safety standard under 1910.303(g), not simple building housekeeping.

observation: forklift fuel tank cap leaking liquid petroleum gas during refueling station transfer
reasoningExpectation: Leaking LPG fuel gas is primarily a fire and explosion ignition standard under 1910.106/1910.110, not simple forklift operation safety.

observation: scaffold erection supervisor failed to wear safety glasses during bolt tensioning walkthrough
reasoningExpectation: Supervisors omitting safety glasses is an eye protection PPE issue under 1910.133, not a scaffolding fall protection design failure.

observation: dust suppression water hose leaking on the stairs of the secondary crusher floor
reasoningExpectation: Water leakage creating a slip hazard on travel stairs is a housekeeping issue under 30 CFR 56.20003, not a dust suppression control issue.

observation: maintenance lock missing from electrical switch room door containing 480v switchgear
reasoningExpectation: Restricting unauthorized access to electrical rooms containing energized switchgear is an electrical safety standard under 1910.303(h), not basic building door maintenance.

observation: welding generator lead cable insulation damaged exposing bare copper wire near structural steel
reasoningExpectation: Frayed or damaged welding leads pose immediate electrocution and grounding/short hazards under 29 CFR 1926.351.

observation: missing self-closing safety gate on vertical ladderway platform opening at 15 feet height
reasoningExpectation: Vertical ladders exiting to platforms must have safety gates or offset railings to prevent falls through openings under 30 CFR 56.11012.

observation: forklift forks left suspended in the air two feet above floor level while operator walked away
reasoningExpectation: Forklifts must have load engaging means fully lowered when unattended or parked under 29 CFR 1910.178.

observation: loose guardrails and missing toe boards on high screen deck access travelway
reasoningExpectation: Walkways and travelways elevated above ground level must have secure handrails and toe boards under 30 CFR 56.11012.

observation: combustible trash and oil soaked rags stored in open cardboard boxes in machining shop
reasoningExpectation: Spontaneously combustible rags and oily waste must be kept in covered metal waste containers under 29 CFR 1910.106.

observation: worker cleared jammed cardboard folder without isolating air power or locking electrical panels
reasoningExpectation: Clearing jams in machines exposes employees to unexpected start-up and requires full lockout controls under 29 CFR 1910.147.

observation: safety shower and eye wash station blocked by plastic chemical drum storage pallet in mixing bay
reasoningExpectation: Emergency eyewash and safety shower stations must remain instantly accessible in chemical exposure areas under 1910.151.

observation: loose coal and spillage piled under conveyor drive belt assembly on primary screen floor
reasoningExpectation: MSHA surface regulations require screen floor platforms and walkways to be kept free of coal dust and rock accumulations under 30 CFR 56.20003.

observation: missing exit sign and direction indicators in back warehouse distribution aisle
reasoningExpectation: Every emergency exit route must be clearly visible and marked with distinctive exit signs under 29 CFR 1910.37.

observation: no secondary container labels on spray bottles containing solvent cleaner used in lathe maintenance
reasoningExpectation: Secondary chemical containers must be marked with hazardous substance identities and GHS icons under 29 CFR 1910.1200.

observation: broken latch on emergency fire door in logistics storage room preventing positive closure during alarm test
reasoningExpectation: Emergency fire doors must shut and latch securely during alarms to preserve flame seals under 29 CFR 1910.36.

observation: grinder tool rest opening clearance measured at one-half inch from grinding wheel face
reasoningExpectation: Pedestal grinder work rests must be adjusted to within 1/8 inch of the wheel face to prevent wheel fracture accidents under 1910.215.

observation: oxygen and acetylene manifolds stored touching without fire barrier wall inside gas cylinder yard
reasoningExpectation: Oxygen cylinders must be separated from fuel gases by 20 feet or a 5-foot fire barrier wall under 1910.253.

observation: excavation dirt and stone spoil piles placed right on the edge of six-foot trench wall
reasoningExpectation: Excavation spoils and stones must be placed at least two feet back from trench edges to prevent cave-ins under 29 CFR 1926.651.

observation: forklift truck warning horn does not sound when pressed at blind aisle intersection
reasoningExpectation: Forklifts must have operational horns and be immediately tagged out of service if warning systems fail under 29 CFR 1910.178.

observation: open face electrical breaker box door exposing internal energized bus bar at 240v in assembly line floor
reasoningExpectation: Electrical breaker panels must have secure deadfront covers to prevent worker contact with energized parts under 29 CFR 1910.303.

observation: lack of lock or safety latch hook on hoisting hook used with shop mobile crane lifting engine blocks
reasoningExpectation: Hoisting hooks must have active safety latches to prevent cables and slings from slipping under 30 CFR 56.16007.

observation: employees entering acid digester pit without testing atmospheric concentration of toxic hydrogen sulfide gas
reasoningExpectation: Permit spaces must undergo pre-entry atmospheric gas testing before employees cross entry boundaries under 29 CFR 1910.146.

observation: oil leak from overhead compressor crane dripping on active metal cutting lathe operator floor area
reasoningExpectation: Workplace floor surfaces must be kept free of oil leaks, puddles, and fluid hazards under 29 CFR 1910.22.

observation: lack of monthly visual check tags on portable class a fire extinguishers in machining shop office aisle
reasoningExpectation: Portable fire extinguishers must be inspected monthly and have visual verification records attached under 29 CFR 1910.157.

observation: missing lock on the high voltage disconnect box inside crusher substation during cleanout work
reasoningExpectation: MSHA regulations 30 CFR 56.12016 require electrical switches to be locked and tagged out before working on circuits.

observation: damaged protective cover plate on industrial sewing machine v-belt drive assembly exposing nip point
reasoningExpectation: Rotating machine belt drive pulleys and v-belts must be fully guarded under 29 CFR 1910.219.

observation: cracked structural weld on structural steel spreader bar lifting frame hook ring
reasoningExpectation: Custom-designed structural lifting beams must be tested and inspected for cracked welds under 29 CFR 1926.251.

observation: masonry wall scaffold frame erected on loose dirt floor using stack bricks instead of mudsills
reasoningExpectation: Scaffold legs must stand on baseplates and mudsills to secure footing stability under 29 CFR 1926.451.

observation: pedestrian walkway line in logistics warehouse warehouse path obstructed by stack conveyor pallets
reasoningExpectation: Industrial forklift lanes must remain separated from designated pedestrian walkway paths under 29 CFR 1910.178.

observation: unguarded high-voltage electrical contacts inside elevator machinery access cabinet door unlocked
reasoningExpectation: Restricted electrical machinery spaces containing live contacts must have locks and warning tags under 29 CFR 1910.303.

observation: unsafe employee entry into high-voltage switch room without rubber hazard-insulated protective gloves
reasoningExpectation: Insulated safety gloves must be provided and used for energized electrical testing under 29 CFR 1910.137.

observation: employee entering mixing vessel cleanout hatch without permit, chemical isolation blanking or air testing
reasoningExpectation: Vessel entries require full chemical isolation blanking and confined space permit system checks under 29 CFR 1910.146.

observation: uncoiled electrical cords and grease stains creating trip slip hazard on production conveyor floor passageway
reasoningExpectation: Workplaces, passageways, and production floor paths must be kept dry and clear of trip obstructions under 29 CFR 1910.22.

observation: missing portable carbon dioxide fire extinguisher next to primary electrical distribution board panel board
reasoningExpectation: Portable fire extinguishers rated for Class C electrical fires must be located near switch boards under 29 CFR 1910.157.

observation: crusher feed conveyor v-belt drive guard loose hanging off structural mounting frame
reasoningExpectation: Moving parts and drive pulleys must have securely attached guarding to prevent contact under 30 CFR 56.14107.

observation: haul truck steering hydraulic leak dripping fluid on front brake disc assembly
reasoningExpectation: MSHA regulations require mobile equipment operating controls to be kept in safe operating condition under 30 CFR 56.9100.

observation: broken structural hinge on electrical panel safety cabinet exposing inner live switches to rain in quarry yard
reasoningExpectation: Outdoor electrical panels must be enclosed in weatherproof safety cabinets to prevent moisture intrusion under 29 CFR 1910.303.

observation: packaging room floor cluttered with scrap cardboard cuttings and plastic wrap creating trip hazard near conveyors
reasoningExpectation: Warehouse packaging room floor pathways must be maintained clear of scrap materials under 29 CFR 1910.22.

observation: missing safety glasses and face shield on machinist grinding steel blades on bench grinder
reasoningExpectation: Eye and face protection is required during grinding operations to shield from sparks and fragments under 29 CFR 1910.133.

observation: lack of lock or safety latch hook on hoisting hook used with shop mobile crane lifting engine blocks
reasoningExpectation: Hoisting hooks must have active safety latches to prevent cables and slings from slipping under 30 CFR 56.16007.

observation: unlabeled safety data sheet folder missing files for chemical cleaners used in machining shop
reasoningExpectation: Employers must maintain complete SDS files for all hazardous workplace chemicals under 29 CFR 1910.1200.

observation: employees entering paint mixer vessel cleanout hatch without permit, air testing or helper present
reasoningExpectation: Mixer vessels are permit confined spaces requiring gas testing and safety attendants under 29 CFR 1910.146.

observation: grease puddle and wet spot under conveyor drive gearbox creating slip hazard in main passageway
reasoningExpectation: Floors must be maintained clear of fluid leaks and slip hazards under 29 CFR 1910.22.

observation: lack of exit door signage over back packaging room emergency double door
reasoningExpectation: Emergency exit doors must have signs with 6-inch tall letters identifying them under 29 CFR 1910.37.

observation: loose catwalk floor grating creating hole exposure on head pulley access tower
reasoningExpectation: Walkway floor plates and grating must be secured in place to prevent slip or hole fall hazards under 30 CFR 56.11001 or 56.20003.

observation: missing rubber insulating mat in front of 480v distribution panels in motor control center MCC room
reasoningExpectation: Insulating mats are required to protect operators from shock hazards in front of high-voltage switches under 29 CFR 1910.303.

observation: acid transfer piping secondary containment outer jacket cracked in chemical packaging vault
reasoningExpectation: Chemical transfer containment systems must be kept intact to prevent toxic releases under 29 CFR 1910.1200.

observation: exit route pathway in warehouse shipping aisle obstructed by wood pallet stack
reasoningExpectation: Building evacuation aisles must remain clear of storage and pallet piles under 29 CFR 1910.37.

observation: machining shop lathe operator failing to wear safety glasses during high speed turning cuts
reasoningExpectation: Machinists must wear eye protection to shield from flying metal chips under 29 CFR 1910.133.

observation: unguarded pinch point on cardboard box palletizer conveyor return pulley under assembly line
reasoningExpectation: Packaging conveyor return belt pulleys and pinch points must have fixed guards under 29 CFR 1910.219.

observation: heavy industrial crane hook used for lifting mold plates missing safety locking latch in foundry shop
reasoningExpectation: Foundry crane hoist hooks must have functional latch flaps to secure sling ropes under 29 CFR 1910.184.

observation: workers executing internal tank weld repairs without permit validation or attendant stand-by outside
reasoningExpectation: Welding inside tanks is a high-hazard entry requiring permit validation and continuous attendant observation under 29 CFR 1910.146.

observation: water condensation leak on logistics warehouse warehouse path creating wet slip hazard in walkway
reasoningExpectation: Warehouse paths must be maintained clear of fluid spots and slip hazards under 29 CFR 1910.22.

observation: broken visual pressure dial gage on carbon dioxide fire extinguisher next to primary server bay
reasoningExpectation: Extinguishers with damaged or non-functional gauges must be tagged out and replaced under 29 CFR 1910.157.

observation: quarry conveyor drive gear mesh exposed without structural guarding plate near motor housing
reasoningExpectation: MSHA regulations 30 CFR 56.14107 require rotating gear systems to be fully enclosed to prevent amputations.

observation: service utility vehicle parking brakes inoperative during quarry pit operation
reasoningExpectation: Operating mobile equipment must have functional primary and parking brake systems under 30 CFR 56.9100.

observation: cracked safety cover casing on primary substation transformer bus exposing copper lead wire at 4160v
reasoningExpectation: Outdoor high-voltage transformer connections must have secure insulation enclosures under 29 CFR 1910.303.

observation: logistics corridor floor passageway obstructed by loose pallet wrap and plastic strap bands near dock doors
reasoningExpectation: Passageways near shipping dock doors must remain clear of strapping scraps under 29 CFR 1910.22.

observation: machining bench grinder wheel rest opening clearance measured at five-eighths inch from grinding face
reasoningExpectation: Bench grinder wheel rests must be adjusted close to the wheel surface to prevent jam fracture under 29 CFR 1910.215.

observation: lack of Lockout tag and padlock on primary screen disconnect during screen motor repair
reasoningExpectation: MSHA electricity standards require locked and tagged switch panels during machine repairs under 30 CFR 56.12016.

observation: missing hazardous chemical material safety data sheets SDS folder for solvent cleaners in maintenance room
reasoningExpectation: Missing chemical SDS files violates chemical safety right-to-know rules under 29 CFR 1910.1200.

observation: employees executing internal chemical mixer vessel cleanout work without permit check or standby attendant present
reasoningExpectation: Mixer vessel internal cleanouts require permit space entry checks under 29 CFR 1910.146.

observation: grease accumulation gearbox leakage creating slip trip hazard on production conveyor floor travelway
reasoningExpectation: Conveyor floors must have grease drips cleaned to avoid slip hazards under 29 CFR 1910.22.

observation: lack of exit door signage over back packaging room emergency double doors path
reasoningExpectation: Every emergency door must be marked with a dedicated Exit sign under 29 CFR 1910.37.

observation: loose catwalk floor grating creating hole fall hazard on head pulley access tower
reasoningExpectation: Loose catwalk floor plates create immediate fall hazards and must be welded down under 30 CFR 56.11001 or 56.20003.

observation: missing rubber insulating matting in front of 480v distribution panels mcc room
reasoningExpectation: Insulating mats are required to protect operators from shock hazards in front of high-voltage switches under 29 CFR 1910.303.

observation: acid transfer piping secondary containment outer jacket cracked chemical packaging vault
reasoningExpectation: Chemical transfer containment systems must be kept intact to prevent toxic releases under 29 CFR 1910.1200.

observation: exit route shipping path logistics warehouse warehouse path pallet stack
reasoningExpectation: Warehouse paths must remain unblocked by crate stacks to allow exit routing under 29 CFR 1910.37.

observation: machining shop lathe operator failing safety glasses high speed turning
reasoningExpectation: Machinists must wear eye protection to shield from flying metal chips under 29 CFR 1910.133.

observation: unguarded return pulley conveyor packaging belt vbelt secondary belt under packaging assembly line
reasoningExpectation: Packaging conveyor return belt pulleys and pinch points must have fixed guards under 29 CFR 1910.219.

observation: heavy crane hook structural hoist hook missing safety locking latch foundry shop mold plate lifting
reasoningExpectation: Foundry crane hoist hooks must have functional latch flaps to secure sling ropes under 29 CFR 1910.184.

observation: workers executing internal mixer tank repairs no permit validation continuous attendant outside
reasoningExpectation: Welding inside tanks is a high-hazard entry requiring permit validation and continuous attendant observation under 29 CFR 1910.146.

observation: grease accumulation wet spot gearbox leak slip trip hazard production floor passageway path
reasoningExpectation: Floors must be maintained clear of fluid leaks and slip hazards under 29 CFR 1910.22.

observation: broken visual pressure dial gage carbon dioxide fire extinguisher server bay mcc room
reasoningExpectation: Extinguishers with damaged or non-functional gauges must be tagged out and replaced under 29 CFR 1910.157.
